description: Scaffold a standalone Fumadocs documentation site at docs-v2/ with a 100% RetroUI neobrutalism design. Every visual element — navbar, sidebar, TOC, search, code blocks, callouts, tabs, cards, headings, buttons — must match the RetroUI design system exactly: 2px black borders, flat offset shadows, sharp corners, Archivo Black headings, Space Grotesk body, yellow primary, physical press interactions. Use actual RetroUI component source code from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main — do not reinvent or approximate.

userStories.title: Scaffold Fumadocs project at docs-v2/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a standalone Fumadocs Next.js project at docs-v2/ so that I have a working documentation site foundation to apply RetroUI theming to.
acceptanceCriteria: docs-v2/ directory exists with its own package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.mjs, postcss.config.mjs
Uses Next.js 15+ App Router with fumadocs-core and fumadocs-ui as dependencies
Uses Tailwind CSS v4 with @tailwindcss/postcss
Has a working docs layout at app/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx
Has a source.ts file using fumadocs-mdx for content loading
Has at least one sample MDX doc page at content/docs/index.mdx
Has mdx-components.tsx with default fumadocs MDX components
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun install && bun run build' succeeds
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run dev' starts a working dev server
userStories.notes: Use 'bunx create-fumadocs-app@latest docs-v2' or manual scaffold. The project must be standalone — NOT part of the opentabs bun workspace. Use pnpm or bun as package manager. Refer to https://fumadocs.dev/docs/ui for setup guidance. Make sure to use fumadocs-ui/css/neutral.css and fumadocs-ui/css/preset.css in the Tailwind CSS file. The project should NOT be added to the root tsconfig.json or any root tooling.

userStories.title: Copy RetroUI component source files into docs-v2/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the actual RetroUI React component source files available in the docs project so that I can use them directly in pages and MDX content.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory docs-v2/components/retroui/ exists with the following component files copied verbatim from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/: Button.tsx, Card.tsx, Badge.tsx, Input.tsx, Textarea.tsx, Dialog.tsx, Accordion.tsx, Alert.tsx, Avatar.tsx, Menu.tsx, Select.tsx, Switch.tsx, Checkbox.tsx, Table.tsx, Text.tsx, Toggle.tsx, ToggleGroup.tsx, Tab.tsx, Tooltip.tsx, Drawer.tsx, Progress.tsx, Popover.tsx, Radio.tsx, Slider.tsx, Sonner.tsx, Label.tsx, Command.tsx, ContextMenu.tsx, Breadcrumb.tsx, Loader.tsx, Empty.tsx, CommandDisplay.tsx, TableOfContents.tsx, IconButton.tsx, index.ts
docs-v2/lib/utils.ts exists with the cn() utility copied verbatim from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/lib/utils.ts
All import paths in the copied components are updated to use the docs-v2 project structure (e.g., @/components/retroui/ and @/lib/utils)

All required npm dependencies for the RetroUI components are installed: @radix-ui/react-accordion, @radix-ui/react-avatar, @radix-ui/react-checkbox, @radix-ui/react-context-menu, @radix-ui/react-dialog, @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu, @radix-ui/react-label, @radix-ui/react-popover, @radix-ui/react-progress, @radix-ui/react-radio-group, @radix-ui/react-select, @radix-ui/react-slider, @radix-ui/react-slot, @radix-ui/react-switch, @radix-ui/react-toggle, @radix-ui/react-toggle-group, @radix-ui/react-tooltip, @radix-ui/react-visually-hidden, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, cmdk, lucide-react, sonner, vaul, @headlessui/react, tw-animate-css
TypeScript compilation of the component files succeeds (no type errors)
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' still succeeds
userStories.notes: Copy files directly from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/. Do NOT copy chart components (BarChart, LineChart, AreaChart, PieChart), BasicCard.tsx, ProductCard.tsx, or Login.tsx — these are demo-only components. Update the import paths: RetroUI uses @/lib/utils and @/components/retroui/Text etc. — the docs-v2 project should use the same @/ alias pointing to docs-v2/. The tsconfig.json in docs-v2 should have paths: { '@/*': ['./*'] }. Do NOT modify the component styling or logic — copy verbatim and only change import paths.

userStories.title: Apply RetroUI global CSS theme (colors, shadows, fonts, radius)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the RetroUI design tokens applied globally so that all components render with the neobrutalist aesthetic — flat offset shadows, 2px black borders, sharp corners, Archivo Black headings, Space Grotesk body text.
acceptanceCriteria: docs-v2/app/global.css contains the EXACT RetroUI @theme inline block with all shadow definitions: --shadow-xs (1px 1px 0 0), --shadow-sm (2px 2px 0 0), --shadow (3px 3px 0 0), --shadow-md (4px 4px 0 0), --shadow-lg (6px 6px 0 0), --shadow-xl (10px 10px 0 1px), --shadow-2xl (16px 16px 0 1px) all using var(--border)

docs-v2/app/global.css contains :root CSS variables matching RetroUI exactly: --background: #fff, --foreground: #000, --primary: #ffdb33, --primary-hover: #ffcc00, --primary-foreground: #000, --secondary: #000, --secondary-foreground: #fff, --card: #fff, --card-foreground: #000, --muted: #d5d5d5, --muted-foreground: #5a5a5a, --accent: #fae583, --accent-foreground: #000, --destructive: #e63946, --destructive-foreground: #fff, --border: #000, --radius: 0

docs-v2/app/global.css contains .dark CSS variables matching RetroUI exactly: --background: #1a1a1a, --foreground: #f5f5f5, --card: #242424, --card-foreground: #f5f5f5, --primary: #ffdb33, --primary-hover: #ffcc00, --primary-foreground: #000, --secondary: #3a3a3a, --secondary-foreground: #f5f5f5, --muted: #3f3f46, --muted-foreground: #a0a0a0, --accent: #fae583, --accent-foreground: #000, --destructive: #e63946, --destructive-foreground: #fff, --border: #5c5c5c

Fumadocs --color-fd-* CSS variables are mapped to the RetroUI variables: --color-fd-background maps to var(--background), --color-fd-foreground maps to var(--foreground), --color-fd-primary maps to var(--primary), --color-fd-primary-foreground maps to var(--primary-foreground), --color-fd-border maps to var(--border), --color-fd-card maps to var(--card), --color-fd-card-foreground maps to var(--card-foreground), --color-fd-muted maps to var(--muted), --color-fd-muted-foreground maps to var(--muted-foreground), --color-fd-accent maps to var(--accent), --color-fd-accent-foreground maps to var(--accent-foreground), --color-fd-secondary maps to var(--secondary), --color-fd-secondary-foreground maps to var(--secondary-foreground), --color-fd-ring maps to var(--border), --color-fd-popover maps to var(--background), --color-fd-popover-foreground maps to var(--foreground)

Three Google Fonts are loaded in the root layout via next/font/google: Archivo_Black (weight 400, variable --font-head), Space_Grotesk (weight 400, variable --font-sans), Space_Mono (weight 400, variable --font-mono). Font CSS variables are applied to <body> className.
body uses font-family: var(--font-sans)
@import 'tw-animate-css' is included in the CSS
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: The global.css in docs-v2 must import fumadocs-ui/css/preset.css (for Fumadocs layout/typography utilities) but should NOT import any fumadocs color preset (no neutral.css, no black.css, etc.) — we provide our own colors via the RetroUI variables. The key bridge is mapping Fumadocs' --color-fd-* tokens to RetroUI's --* tokens. Copy the @theme inline block exactly from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/app/global.css. Add @import 'tw-animate-css' for the animation utilities RetroUI components depend on. The Fumadocs preset must still be imported so that its prose, layout utilities, and fd-* utility classes work. Use @import 'fumadocs-ui/css/preset.css' AFTER the RetroUI theme variables so Fumadocs picks up our colors.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs prose/typography with RetroUI styling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MDX content (paragraphs, headings, lists, links, blockquotes, inline code, tables) to render with RetroUI typography — Archivo Black for headings, Space Grotesk for body, 2px borders on tables, neobrutalist link styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Headings (h1-h6) in MDX content use font-head (Archivo Black) with the same size/weight scale as RetroUI Text component: h1 = text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-bold, h2 = text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-semibold, h3 = text-2xl font-medium, h4 = text-xl font-normal, h5 = text-lg font-normal, h6 = text-base font-normal
Paragraph text uses font-sans (Space Grotesk) with text-base
Links in prose have hover:underline underline-offset-2 decoration-primary matching RetroUI Text 'a' variant
Code blocks and inline code use font-mono (Space Mono)
Tables in MDX content have border-2 shadow-lg matching RetroUI Table component: header row has bg-primary text-primary-foreground font-head, rows have border-b and hover:bg-primary/50
Blockquotes have border-l-4 border-primary with bg-accent/20 padding
Lists use font-sans with proper spacing
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: This is done by overriding the Fumadocs prose styles. Fumadocs uses a built-in Tailwind CSS Typography plugin that adds a 'prose' class. We need to either: (1) Override the prose variants in CSS to match RetroUI styles, or (2) Override individual MDX components in mdx-components.tsx to use RetroUI's Text component and styling. Approach (2) is preferred because it gives exact control. In mdx-components.tsx, map h1-h6 to custom components that use the RetroUI Text component's exact classes. Map table/thead/tbody/tr/th/td to use RetroUI Table's exact classes. Map a to use RetroUI Text 'a' variant classes. Reference the exact Tailwind classes from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Text.tsx and Table.tsx.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Callout/Card MDX components with RetroUI Alert/Card
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Fumadocs callout and card components in MDX to render with the RetroUI design — 2px borders, flat offset shadows, sharp corners, primary color accents.
acceptanceCriteria: Fumadocs <Callout> in MDX renders with RetroUI Alert styling: border-2, rounded (radius 0 by default), p-4, appropriate status variant colors (info = blue, warn = yellow, error = red) matching RetroUI Alert.tsx
Fumadocs <Card> in MDX renders with RetroUI Card styling: border-2, shadow-md, transition-all, hover:shadow-none, bg-card matching RetroUI Card.tsx
Card title uses font-head (Archivo Black)
Card description uses text-muted-foreground font-sans
Cards have the physical press hover effect: shadow reduces on hover
A sample MDX page demonstrates both Callout variants (info, warn, error) and Card components to verify visual correctness
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: In mdx-components.tsx, override the Fumadocs Callout component to use RetroUI Alert styling. Override the Fumadocs Card components to use RetroUI Card styling. Read the exact component code from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Alert.tsx and Card.tsx. The Fumadocs callout has a 'type' prop (info, warn, error) which maps to RetroUI Alert 'status' prop (info, warning, error). You may need to create wrapper components that adapt the Fumadocs API to RetroUI's visual output. Do NOT change the MDX authoring API — authors should still write <Callout type='info'> and <Card> — just change the rendered output.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs code blocks with RetroUI-styled code blocks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want code blocks in MDX to render with RetroUI neobrutalist styling — 2px borders, flat offset shadows, dark background, title bar with primary color accents.
acceptanceCriteria: Code blocks (```code```) render with border-2 border-border, shadow-md (flat offset shadow), and sharp corners (radius 0)
Code block title bar (when title is specified via ```ts title='file.ts'```) has bg-primary text-primary-foreground font-head styling, matching RetroUI Dialog.Header pattern: border-b-2 px-4 min-h-10
Code block copy button has RetroUI button styling: border-2, shadow-sm hover:shadow-none, transition-all
Inline code has bg-muted border border-border px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono styling
Code font is Space Mono (var(--font-mono))
A sample MDX page demonstrates code blocks with and without titles, inline code, and multi-language examples
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: Eject the Fumadocs codeblock component using 'npx @fumadocs/cli add codeblock' (run from within docs-v2/), then modify the ejected source to apply RetroUI styling. Alternatively, override the 'pre' component in mdx-components.tsx. The key visual elements: outer container gets border-2 + shadow-md (RetroUI's flat shadows), the title bar mimics RetroUI Dialog.Header (bg-primary with border-b-2), copy button uses RetroUI Button outline variant classes. Reference ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Dialog.tsx for header styling and Button.tsx for button styling.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Tabs with RetroUI Tabs styling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Fumadocs Tab components in MDX to render with RetroUI's tab design — bordered triggers with primary color on selection, bordered content panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab triggers have px-4 py-1 border-2 border-transparent styling in unselected state
Selected tab trigger has border-border bg-primary text-primary-foreground font-semibold matching RetroUI Tab.tsx TabsTrigger
Tab content panel has border-2 border-border mt-2 p-4 w-full matching RetroUI Tab.tsx TabsContent
Tab trigger list has flex flex-row space-x-2 layout
A sample MDX page demonstrates tabs with multiple panels to verify visual correctness
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: Eject Fumadocs tabs using 'npx @fumadocs/cli add tabs' from docs-v2/, then modify the styling to match RetroUI Tab component. Read the exact classes from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Tab.tsx. Note: RetroUI uses @headlessui/react for tabs while Fumadocs uses Radix UI — we should keep Fumadocs' Radix-based tabs but apply RetroUI's visual classes. This is a styling-only change, not a component replacement.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Accordion with RetroUI Accordion styling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Fumadocs accordion components to render with RetroUI's accordion design — bordered items with flat shadows, primary color accents, physical shadow interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Accordion items have border-2 bg-background rounded shadow-md hover:shadow-sm transition-all overflow-hidden matching RetroUI Accordion.tsx
Accordion header trigger has flex items-start justify-between px-4 py-2 font-head cursor-pointer with chevron icon that rotates 180 degrees when open
Accordion content has font-sans body text styling
Open state reduces shadow (data-[state=open]:shadow-sm)
A sample MDX page demonstrates single and multiple accordion panels
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: Eject Fumadocs accordion using 'npx @fumadocs/cli add accordion' from docs-v2/, then restyle to match RetroUI. Read exact classes from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Accordion.tsx. Both RetroUI and Fumadocs use @radix-ui/react-accordion so the underlying primitive is the same — this is purely a visual restyling.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Files (file tree) with RetroUI styling

acceptanceCriteria: Files container has border-2 shadow-md with sharp corners matching RetroUI Card pattern
Folder items use font-head for names, with primary color folder icon
File items use font-sans for names
Interactive folder expand/collapse has appropriate hover states

userStories.notes: Eject Fumadocs files using 'npx @fumadocs/cli add files' from docs-v2/, then restyle. Apply RetroUI Card-like container styling (border-2, shadow-md) and use primary color for interactive elements.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Navbar with RetroUI neobrutalist navbar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the top navigation bar to look like RetroUI — 2px bottom border, primary color accents, Archivo Black logo text, physical button interactions for nav items.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar has border-b-2 border-border bg-background
Site logo/title uses font-head (Archivo Black) with text-xl or larger
Navigation links use font-sans with hover:bg-primary/20 or hover:text-primary transition
Search toggle button has RetroUI button styling: border-2, shadow-sm, hover:shadow-none, transition-all with press effect (hover:translate-y-0.5)
Theme toggle button has RetroUI button styling matching the search toggle
GitHub icon link (if present) has RetroUI IconButton styling: border-2, shadow-sm hover:shadow-none
Mobile hamburger menu trigger has RetroUI button styling
The navbar is visually consistent with RetroUI's overall design language
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: Use the Fumadocs DocsLayout nav.component prop to provide a custom navbar, OR use CSS overrides on the default Fumadocs navbar. If using CSS overrides: target the Fumadocs navbar elements via their CSS classes (inspect the rendered HTML). If building a custom component: use 'npx @fumadocs/cli customise' to eject the navbar layout. Apply RetroUI Button.tsx classes to interactive elements (border-2, shadow-sm, hover effects). Reference RetroUI IconButton.tsx for icon-only buttons. The navbar should feel like it belongs in the RetroUI component library.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Sidebar with RetroUI neobrutalist sidebar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs sidebar to render with RetroUI styling — bordered container, primary color active states, Archivo Black section headers, flat shadows on interactive elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar container has border-r-2 border-border bg-background (or bg-card in dark mode)
Sidebar section/group headers use font-head
Active sidebar link has bg-primary text-primary-foreground font-semibold
Inactive sidebar links have hover:bg-accent/50 transition
Sidebar folder expand/collapse icons match the RetroUI Accordion chevron pattern
Sidebar separator lines use border-border
The sidebar search input (if present) has RetroUI Input styling: border-2, shadow-md, focus:shadow-xs
Overall sidebar is visually indistinguishable from what you would expect in a RetroUI application

userStories.notes: Use 'npx @fumadocs/cli customise' to eject the sidebar, or apply CSS overrides. The Fumadocs sidebar renders a navigation tree with collapsible folders. Apply RetroUI styling to: container (border-r-2), active link (bg-primary), section headers (font-head), input (RetroUI Input.tsx classes). Read the RetroUI Input.tsx classes from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Input.tsx: px-4 py-2 w-full rounded border-2 shadow-md transition focus:outline-hidden focus:shadow-xs.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Table of Contents with RetroUI styling

Fumadocs TOC has active heading tracking built in.

userStories.title: Override Fumadocs Search Dialog with RetroUI Command styling

Fumadocs provides lower-level search dialog components (SearchDialog, SearchDialogContent, SearchDialogHeader, SearchDialogInput, SearchDialogList, etc.) from fumadocs-ui/components/dialog/search that can be restyled.

Pages should use placeholder content but realistic structure. Import RetroUI components directly where needed (e.g., <Badge>, <Button>) and use Fumadocs MDX components (Callout, Card, Tabs, etc.) which should already be restyled from previous stories.

userStories.title: Final visual audit and polish pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every visual element on the documentation site to be indistinguishable from what RetroUI would render — no default Fumadocs styling leaking through anywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Every border on the site is 2px solid (border-2) — no 1px borders from default Fumadocs
Every shadow is a flat offset shadow with 0 blur — no blurred/soft shadows from default Fumadocs
Every corner is sharp (radius 0) unless explicitly rounded — no rounded-lg or rounded-xl from Fumadocs defaults
Every heading uses font-head (Archivo Black) — no default system fonts or Inter
Every body text uses font-sans (Space Grotesk) — no default sans-serif
Every code element uses font-mono (Space Mono)
Primary color (#ffdb33 yellow) is used consistently for: active states, selected states, primary buttons, dialog headers, table headers, hover accents
Dark mode works correctly with the RetroUI dark theme colors
Interactive elements (buttons, links, cards, accordion items) have the physical press effect: shadow shrinks + element translates on hover/active
No default Fumadocs blue/purple accent colors are visible anywhere
Mobile responsive layout works correctly
Running 'cd docs-v2 && bun run build' succeeds
userStories.notes: This is the final polish pass. Go through every page and every component, checking for any visual elements that still look like default Fumadocs rather than RetroUI. Common things to watch: focus rings (should use --border color), scrollbar styling, tooltip/popover backgrounds, skeleton/loading states, empty states. Fix any remaining CSS leaks by adding more specific overrides. The goal is that if someone who knows RetroUI looks at this site, they would say 'this is RetroUI' without hesitation.
